# ontology-extractor

TypeScript library for extracting a draft ontology TBox from document corpora. The core package is provider-agnostic: callers inject structured-output LLM and embedding adapters.

## Workspace

- `packages/ontology-extractor`: extraction pipeline and public contracts.
- `examples/ontology-report`: offline CLI sample and static HTML report.
- `examples/ontology-report`: also contains the local upload web application.
- `spec.md`: normative MVP behavior.

## Verify

<!-- Generated by GitHub Copilot -->
```sh
pnpm install
pnpm test
pnpm typecheck
pnpm build
pnpm test:e2e
```

## Documentation

The detailed Docusaurus documentation lives in `packages/docs`.

<!-- Generated by GitHub Copilot -->
```sh
pnpm --filter ontology-docs start
pnpm --filter ontology-docs build
```

## CLI sample

The default provider is a deterministic demonstration adapter, so the first run does not send documents to an external service. It recognizes a small software-architecture vocabulary (`Service`, `API`, `Database`, `exposes`, `uses`) and is intended to exercise the pipeline, not measure model quality.

<!-- Generated by GitHub Copilot -->
```sh
pnpm build
pnpm --filter ontology-report start -- \
	--provider mock \
	--input ./documents \
	--output ./report
```

Add `--refine ./more-documents` to demonstrate cumulative support. The CLI writes `ontology.json`, `run.json`, `index.html`, and the local `report-client.js` bundle. The report opens directly in a browser, requires no CDN, and provides an interactive Cytoscape graph plus an equivalent accessible table.

To evaluate an extraction against an expected TBox, pass a previously curated ontology JSON. The CLI calculates deterministic precision, recall, F1, false positives, and false negatives for node types and relations; no evaluator LLM call is made.

<!-- Generated by GitHub Copilot -->
```sh
pnpm --filter ontology-report start -- \
	--provider mock \
	--input ./documents \
	--gold ./expected-ontology.json \
	--output ./evaluated-report
```

Type matching is case-insensitive and accepts canonical aliases. Relation matching compares normalized relation label, domain label, and range label. The gold file uses the same `Ontology` JSON shape emitted by the library.

### LM Studio

Start an OpenAI-compatible LM Studio server with both a chat model supporting structured output/tool calling and an embedding model. Set the actual vector dimension returned by the embedding model.

<!-- Generated by GitHub Copilot -->
```sh
export LMSTUDIO_BASE_URL="http://localhost:1234/v1"
export LMSTUDIO_CHAT_MODEL="your-loaded-chat-model"
export LMSTUDIO_EMBEDDING_MODEL="your-loaded-embedding-model"
export ONTOLOGY_EMBEDDING_DIMENSIONS="768"

pnpm --filter ontology-report start -- \
	--provider lmstudio \
	--input ./documents \
	--output ./report-lmstudio
```

`LMSTUDIO_API_KEY` is optional and defaults to the non-secret placeholder `lm-studio`.

### Azure OpenAI

Use separate chat and embedding deployment names. Credentials are read only from environment variables and are not included in `run.json` or the HTML report.

<!-- Generated by GitHub Copilot -->
```sh
export AZURE_OPENAI_API_KEY="..."
export AZURE_OPENAI_ENDPOINT="https://your-resource.openai.azure.com"
export AZURE_OPENAI_API_VERSION="2024-10-21"
export AZURE_OPENAI_CHAT_DEPLOYMENT="ontology-chat"
export AZURE_OPENAI_EMBEDDING_DEPLOYMENT="ontology-embeddings"
export ONTOLOGY_EMBEDDING_DIMENSIONS="1536"

pnpm --filter ontology-report start -- \
	--provider azure \
	--input ./documents \
	--output ./report-azure
```

When `lmstudio` or `azure` is selected, document fragments and ontology prompts are sent to that provider. Review data-classification and retention requirements before processing sensitive corpora.

## Web application

The web application uses Fastify, a framework-free TypeScript browser client, and Cytoscape. Provider credentials and deployment settings remain on the server. Jobs and ontology results are held in memory and expire after 30 minutes by default; uploaded document text is not persisted.

<!-- Generated by GitHub Copilot -->
```sh
pnpm build
pnpm --filter ontology-report start:web
```

Open `http://127.0.0.1:3000`. The default `ONTOLOGY_PROVIDER=mock` runs entirely offline. To use LM Studio or Azure, set `ONTOLOGY_PROVIDER` and the provider variables described above in `examples/ontology-report/.env`.

The MVP accepts up to 20 TXT, Markdown, or HTML files, with a default limit of 2 MiB per file and 20 MiB in total. The UI provides upload/drop, live SSE progress, cancellation, an interactive graph, an accessible canonical-type table, formatted JSON, and JSON download.

This local sample has no authentication and binds to `127.0.0.1` by default. Do not expose it directly to the internet. PDF parsing, persistent multi-user storage, and cross-session refinement are outside the MVP scope.
